pattern: Split-then-fixed-window: first cut the string wherever the adjacency rule breaks so no candidate substring can ever cross that seam, then in each remaining chunk slide a window whose length is fixed by the target distinct-character count so you can track 'each letter seen exactly k times' with running counters instead of recomputing from scratch.
hints: A complete substring's adjacent letters can never differ by more than two alphabet positions, so first split the whole string at every place that rule is violated — no valid answer can straddle a split point.

Inside one of those pieces, if a complete substring uses exactly d distinct letters, its length is forced to be exactly d * k, since every one of those d letters must appear exactly k times — so for each d from 1 to 26 you only need to check windows of that one fixed length.

Slide a fixed-length window across the piece maintaining a 26-slot frequency table plus two running counters — how many letters currently have nonzero count, and how many currently have a count of exactly k — so each slide step is O(1) instead of rescanning the whole window.

edge_inputs.description: three distinct characters each already appearing exactly once
an alphabet gap greater than two must break any cross-boundary substring
a repeated character where only unit-length windows can satisfy k=1
two letters each repeated k times, mixed so only some windows line up
word length exactly equal to k, exactly one full-length window is possible
a full ascending run of all 26 letters, each appearing once, maximizing distinct-character windows
three distinct letters each repeated k times back to back

stress.description: large random string over a small contiguous alphabet window at max length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    letters = 'abcde'
    word = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(size))
    k = rng.randint(1, 5)
    return [word, k]

stress.description: highly repetitive block pattern stressing the sliding-window counters across many distinct-count values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    block = 'aabbccddeeff'
    reps = size // len(block) + 1
    word = (block * reps)[:size]
    k = 2
    return [word, k]
